text:
Sunscreen (also called sunblock) is a product you apply to your skin to protect it from the harmful effects of the sun’s ultraviolet (UV) radiation.

What it does

Sunlight contains UV rays that can:

Damage your skin cells
Cause sunburn
Speed up aging (wrinkles, dark spots)
Increase the risk of skin cancer

Sunscreen helps by either absorbing or reflecting these UV rays before they harm your skin.There are two main kinds:

Physical (mineral): Contains ingredients like zinc oxide or titanium dioxide that sit on top of the skin and reflect UV rays
Chemical: Absorbs UV rays and converts them into heat
What SPF means

SPF stands for Sun Protection Factor.

SPF 15 blocks about 93% of UVB rays
SPF 30 blocks about 97%
SPF 50 blocks about 98%

Higher SPF gives slightly more protection, but no sunscreen blocks 100%.

How to use it
Apply 15–20 minutes before going outside
Use enough to cover all exposed skin
Reapply every 2 hours (or after swimming/sweating)
